No Poverty.reason: The course covers 'Monetary and fiscal policy' and 'international finance markets', which are tools that can be used to create policy frameworks for poverty reduction (Target 1.b).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course analyzes 'macroeconomic activity' and 'international finance markets', which are fundamental to sustaining economic growth and strengthening financial institutions (Targets 8.1, 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The study of 'international finance markets' and 'international money markets' provides the theoretical foundation for financial services that support infrastructure development and industry (Targets 9.1, 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers 'Monetary and fiscal policy' and 'international finance markets', which are key tools for addressing inequality and regulating global markets (Targets 10.4, 10.5).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Understanding 'international finance markets' and 'Central bank intervention' is foundational for developing transparent institutions and tackling illicit financial flows (Targets 16.4, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is central to understanding global macroeconomic stability and international finance, which are core components of this goal (Targets 17.1, 17.13, 17.14).
